We are using the edismax parser.\\n243:         # See https://solr.apache.org/guide/8_11/the-extended-dismax-query-parser.html\\n244:         # This is somewhat synonymous to setting defType=edismax in the\\n245:         # query, but much more flexible. We wouldn't be able to do our\\n246:         # complicated parent/child queries with defType!\\n247: \\n248:         full_work_query = '({{!edismax q.op=\\\"AND\\\" qf=\\\"{qf}\\\" bf=\\\"{bf}\\\" v={v}}})'.format(\\n249:             # qf: the fields to query un-prefixed parts of the query.\\n250:             # e.g. 'harry potter' becomes\\n251:             # 'text:(harry potter) OR alternative_title:(harry potter)^20 OR ...'\\n252:             qf='text alternative_title^20 author_name^20',\\n253:             # bf (boost factor): boost results based on the value of this\\n254:             # field. I.e. results with more editions get boosted, upto a\\n255:             # max of 100, after which we don't see it as good signal of\\n256:             # quality.\\n257:             bf='min(100,edition_count)',\\n258:             # v: the query to process with the edismax query parser.   def q_to_solr_params(self, q: str, solr_fields: set[str]) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\\n233:         params: list[tuple[str, str]] = []\\n234: \\n235:         # We need to parse the tree so that it way the search is processed. We are using the edismax parser.\\n243:         # See https://solr.apache.org/guide/8_11/the-extended-dismax-query-parser.html\\n244:         # This is somewhat synonymous to setting defType=edismax in the\\n245:         # query, but much more flexible. We wouldn't be able to do our\\n246:         # complicated parent/child queries with defType!\\n247: \\n248:         full_work_query = '({{!edismax q.op=\\\"AND\\\" qf=\\\"{qf}\\\" bf=\\\"{bf}\\\" v={v}}})'.format(\\n249:             # qf: the fields to query un-prefixed parts of the query.\\n250:             # e.g. 'harry potter' becomes\\n251:             # 'text:(harry potter) OR alternative_title:(harry potter)^20 OR ...'\\n252:             qf='text alternative_title^20 author_name^20',\\n253:             # bf (boost factor): boost results based on the value of this\\n254:             # field. I.e. results with more editions get boosted, upto a\\n255:             # max of 100, after which we don't see it as good signal of\\n256:             # quality.\\n257:             bf='min(100,edition_count)',\\n258:             # v: the query to process with the edismax query parser. Note\\n259:             # we are using a solr variable here; this reads the url parameter\\n260:             # arbitrarily called workQuery.\\n261:             v='$workQuery',\\n262:         )\\n263: \\n264:         ed_q = None\\n265:         editions_fq = []\\n266:         if has_solr_editions_enabled() and 'editions:[subquery]' in solr_fields:\\n267:             WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD = {\\n268:                 # If the tree\\n14:     ends up being empty, errors.\\n15: \\n16:     :param child: Node to remove\\n17:     :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\\n18:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n19:     parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\\n20:     if parent is None:\\n21:         # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\\n22:         raise EmptyTreeError()\\n23:     elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\\n24:         new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\\n25:         if not new_children:\\n26:             # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\\n27:             # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\\n28:             luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\\n29:         else:\\n30:             parent.children = new_children\\n31:     else:\\n32:         raise ValueError(\\\"Not supported for generic class Item\\\")\\n33: \\n34: \\n35: def luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\\n36:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n37:     Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\\n38: \\n39:     Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\\n40:     modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\\n41: \\n42:     :param item: Node to traverse\\n43:     :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\\n44:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n45:     parents = _parents or []\\n46:     yield item, parents\\n47:     new_parents = [*parents, item]\\n48:     for child in item.children:\\n49:         yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\\n50: \\n51: \\n52: def escape_unknown_fields(\\n53:     query: str,\\n54:     is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\\n55:     lower=True,\\n56: ) -> str:\\n57:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n58:     Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\\n59:     predicate function `is_valid_field`.\\n60: \\n61:     :param query: Query to escape\\n62:     :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\\n63:     :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\\n64: \\n65:     >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\\n66:     'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\\n67:     >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar   blah:bar baz:boo', lambda field: False)\\n68:     'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar   blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\\n69:     >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar', {'title'}.__contains__)\\n70:     'title:foo bar'\\n71:     >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'title'}.__contains__)\\n72:     'title:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\\n73:     >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'TITLE'}.__contains__, lower=False)\\n74:     'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\\n75:     >>> escape_unknown_fields('hi', {'title'}.__contains__)\\n76:     'hi'\\n77:     >>> escape_unknown_fields('(title:foo) OR (blah:bah)', {'title'}.__contains__)\\n78:     '(title:foo) OR (blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bah)'\\n79:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n80:     tree = parser.parse(query)\\n81:     # Note we use the string of the tree, because it strips spaces\\n82:     # like: \\\"title : foo\\\" -> \\\"title:foo\\\"\\n83:     escaped_query = str(tree)\\n84:     offset = 0\\n85:     for sf, _ in luqum_traverse(tree):\\n86:         if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(\\n87:             sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name\\n88:         ):\\n89:             field = sf.name + r'\\\\:'\\n90:             if hasattr(sf, 'head'):\\n91:                 # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;\\n92:                 # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing\\n93:                 # together\\n94:                 field = sf.head + field\\n95: \\n96:             # We will be moving left to right, so we need to adjust the offset\\n97:             # to account for the characters we have already replaced\\n98:             escaped_query = string.\\n110: \\n111:     >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo')\\n112:     'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\\n113:     >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\\n114:     'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\n130: def luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\\n131:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n132:     Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\\n133: \\n134:     In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\\n135:     affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\\n136: \\n137:     Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\\n138:     parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\\n139: \\n140:     Query : title:foo bar\\n141:     Lucene: (title:foo) bar\\n142:     OL    : (title:foo bar)\\n143: \\n144:     Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\\n145:     Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\\n146:     OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\\n147: \\n148:     This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\\n149:     Luqum parser, unfortunately.\\n150: \\n151:     Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\\n152:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n153:     tree = parser.parse(query)\\n154: \\n155:     def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\\n156:         if isinstance(item, Word):\\n157:             return item, None\\n158:         elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\\n159:             return item.children[0], item\\n160:         else:\\n161:             return None\\n162: \\n163:     for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\\n164:         if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\\n165:             # (blah OR author:bar)\\n187:                         # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\\n188:                         node.op = parent_op.op\\n189:                         node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\\n190:                     to_rem.append(child)\\n191:                 else:\\n192:                     last_sf = None\\n193:             if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\\n194:                 # We only have the searchfield left!\\n195:                 if parents:\\n196:                     # Move the head to the next element\\n197:                     last_sf.head = node.head\\n198:                     parents[-1].children = tuple(\\n199:                         child if child is not node else last_sf\\n200:                         for child in parents[-1].children\\n201:                     )\\n202:                 else:\\n203:                     tree = last_sf\\n204:                     break\\n205:             else:\\n206:                 node.children = tuple(\\n207:                     child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\\n208:                 )\\n209: \\n210:     # Remove spaces before field names\\n211:     for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\\n212:         if isinstance(node, SearchField):\\n213:             node.expr.head = ''\\n214: \\n215:     return tree\\n216: \\n217: \\n218: def query_dict_to_str(\\n219:     escaped: dict | None = None,\\n220:     unescaped: dict | None = None,\\n221:     op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\\n222: ) -> str:\\n223:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n224:     Converts a query dict to a search query.\\n225: \\n226:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\\n227:     'title:(foo)'\\n228:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\\n229:     'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\\n230:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\\n231:     'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\\n232:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\\n233:     'title:(foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\? to escape)'\\n234:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\\n235:     'title:(YES and)'\\n236:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n237:     result = ''\\n238:     if escaped:\\n239:         result += f' {op} '.join(\\n240:             f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\\n241:         )\\n242:     if unescaped:\\n243:         if result:\\n244:             result += f' {op} '\\n245:         result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\\n246:     return result\\n247: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/openlibrary--7f6b722a@7f6b722a10f822171501d027cad60afe53337732/-/blob/openlibrary/solr/query_utils.py\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a398a29e-e698-4f91-b5cd-2af9bc40715a","uuid":"3120352f-80e7-4c6c-8375-8e052b750a4b","tool_use_result":{"content":"{\"content\":\"1: from typing import Callable, Literal, Optional\\n2: from luqum.parser import parser\\n3: from luqum.tree import Item, SearchField, BaseOperation, Group, Word, Unary\\n4: import re\\n5: \\n6: \\n7: class EmptyTreeError(Exception):\\n8:     pass\\n9: \\n10: \\n11: def luqum_remove_child(child: Item, parents: list[Item]):\\n12:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n13:     Removes a child from a luqum parse tree. hit.\n136: \n137:     Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n138:     parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n139: \n140:     Query : title:foo bar\n141:     Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n142:     OL    : (title:foo bar)\n143: \n144:     Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n145:     Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n146:     OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n147: \n148:     This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n149:     Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n150: \n151:     Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n152:     \"\"\"\n153:     tree = parser.parse(query)\n154: \n155:     def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n156:         if isinstance(item, Word):\n157:             return item, None\n158:         elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n159:             return item.children[0], item\n160:         else:\n161:             return None\n162: \n163:     for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n164:         if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n165:             # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n166:             # we bundle them together\n167:             last_sf: SearchField = None\n168:             to_rem = []\n169:             for child in node.children:\n170:                 if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n171:                     last_sf = child\n172:                 elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n173:                     word, parent_op = next_word\n174:                     # Add it over\n175:                     if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n176:                         last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n177:                         last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n178:                         word.tail = ''\n179:                     else:\n180:                         last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n181:                         last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n182:                         last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n183:                         word.tail = ''\n184:                     if parent_op:\n185:                         # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n186:                         # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n187:                         # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n188:                         node.op = parent_op.op\n189:                         node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n190:                     to_rem.append(child)\n191:                 else:\n192:                     last_sf = None\n193:             if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n194:                 # We only have the searchfield left!\n195:                 if parents:\n196:                     # Move the head to the next element\n197:                     last_sf.head = node.head\n198:                     parents[-1].children = tuple(\n199:                         child if child is not node else last_sf\n200:                         for child in parents[-1].children\n201:                     )\n202:                 else:\n203:                     tree = last_sf\n204:                     break\n205:             else:\n206:                 node.children = tuple(\n207:                     child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n208:                 )\n209: \n210:     # Remove spaces before field names\n211:     for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n212:         if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n213:             node.expr.head = ''\n214: \n215:     return tree\n216: \n217: \n218: def query_dict_to_str(\n219:     escaped: dict | None = None,\n220:     unescaped: dict | None = None,\n221:     op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n222: ) -> str:\n223:     \"\"\"\n224:     Converts a query dict to a search query.\n225: \n226:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n227:     'title:(foo)'\n228:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n229:     'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n230:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n231:     'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n232:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n233:     'title:(foo \\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n234:     >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n235:     'title:(YES and)'\n236:     \"\"\"\n237:     result = ''\n238:     if escaped:\n239:         result += f' {op} '.join(\n240:             f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\n241:         )\n242:     if unescaped:\n243:         if result:\n244:             result += f' {op} '\n245:         result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n246:     return result\n247: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/openlibrary--7f6b722a@7f6b722a10f822171501d027cad60afe53337732/-/blob/openlibrary/solr/query_utils.py"}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01BhqACp9FHxjhZYmQVr3c66","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Perfect! Now I can see the issue clearly. Looking at line 124 of `query_utils.py`:\n\n```python\nescaped = re.sub(r'[\\\\[\\\\]\\\\(\\\\)\\\\{\\\\}:\\\"\\\\-+?~^/\\\\\\\\,]', r'\\\\\\\\\\\\g<0>', escaped)\n```\n\nThe `fully_escape_query` function already escapes dashes (`-`) with the regex pattern. But the problem is in the `process_user_query` method of the SearchScheme class!\n\nLooking at lines 71-91 of `schemes/__init__.py`:\n\n```python\ntry:\n    q_param = escape_unknown_fields(\n        (\n            # Solr 4+ has support for regexes (eg `key:/foo.*/`)! But for now,\n            # let's not expose that and escape all '/'. Otherwise\n            # `key:/works/OL1W` is interpreted as a regex.\n            q_param.strip()\n            .replace('/', '\\\\\\\\/')\n            # Also escape unexposed lucene features\n            .replace('?', '\\\\\\\\?')\n            .replace('~', '\\\\\\\\~')\n        ),\n        self.is_search_field,\n        lower=True,\n    )\n    q_tree = luqum_parser(q_param)\nexcept ParseError:\n    # This isn't a syntactically valid lucene query\n    logger.warning(\"Invalid lucene query\", exc_info=True)\n    # Escape everything we can\n    q_tree = luqum_parser(fully_escape_query(q_param))\n```\n\nThe issue is that before `escape_unknown_fields` is called, the code escapes `/`, `?`, and `~`, but NOT `-` (dash). This means that a trailing dash like in `Horror-` will be passed to `escape_unknown_fields` unescaped. When this gets passed to the Lucene parser, it can cause issues because `-` is a special character in Lucene (it means negation/exclusion).\n\n\nThe test expects `'foo foo bar -'` to become `'foo foo bar \\\\\\\\-'`, so the dash needs escaping. The fix is straightforward: add `.replace('-', '\\\\\\\\-')` alongside the other character replacements to handle dashes before they reach the parser.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3659,"cache_read_input_tokens":50282,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3659},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a398a29e-e698-4f91-b5cd-2af9bc40715a","uuid":"94f3b31c-949d-4f26-a328-f28a5336bfd7"}

Prefix\\n19:         (?P<prestar>\\\\*)?  # Should be suffix\\n20:         (?P<neg>-)?  # Old standard; no longer used\\n21:         (?P<j>j)?  # Juvenile prefix\\n22:         C?  # Canadian CIP\\n23: \\n24:         # The number\\n25:         (?P<number>\\\\d{1,3}(\\\\.+\\\\s?\\\\d+)?)\\n26: \\n27:         # Suffix\\n28:         (?P<poststar>\\\\*?)\\n29:         (?P<s>\\\\s?s)?  # Series suffix\\n30:         (?P<B>\\\\s?\\\\[?B\\\\]?)?  # Biographical\\n31:         (?P<ninetwo>\\\\s(092|920|92))?  # No clue; shouldn't be its own DDC though\\n32:     )\\n33:     |\\n34:     (\\\\[?(?P<fic>Fic|E)\\\\.?\\\\]?)\\n35: ''',\\n36:     re.IGNORECASE | re.X,\\n37: )\\n38: \\n39: \\n40: def collapse_multiple_space(s: str) -> str:\\n41:     return MULTIPLE_SPACES_RE.sub(' ', s)\\n42: \\n43: \\n44: VALID_CHARS = set(printable) - set(\\\"/'′’,\\\")\\n45: \\n46: \\n47: def normalize_ddc(ddc: str) -> list[str]:\\n48:     ddc = ''.join(\\n49:         char for char in collapse_multiple_space(ddc.strip()) if char in VALID_CHARS\\n50:     )\\n51: \\n52:     results: list[str] = []\\n53:     for match in DDC_RE.finditer(ddc):\\n54:         parts = match.groupdict()\\n55:         prefix = ''\\n56:         suffix = ''\\n57: \\n58:         # DDCs should start at word boundaries\\n59:         start = match.start()\\n60:         if start > 0 and re.search(r'\\\\b', ddc[start - 1]):\\n61:             continue\\n62:         # And end at them\\n63:         end = match.end()\\n64:         if end < len(ddc) and re.search(r'\\\\b', ddc[end]):\\n65:             continue\\n66: \\n67:         # Some old standard which isn't used anymore; might need to filter these\\n68:         # out, but they should sort OK so let's keep them.\\n69:         if parts['neg']:\\n70:             prefix += '-'\\n71:         # Juvenile prefix\\n72:         if parts['j']:\\n73:             prefix += 'j'\\n74: \\n75:         # Star should be at end\\n76:         if parts['prestar'] or parts['poststar']:\\n77:             suffix = '*'\\n78:         # Series suffix\\n79:         if parts['s']:\\n80:             suffix += ' s'\\n81:         # Biographical\\n82:         if parts['B']:\\n83:             suffix += ' B'\\n84:         # Not at all sure\\n85:         if parts['ninetwo']:\\n86:             suffix += parts['ninetwo']\\n87: \\n88:         # And now the actual number!\\n89:         if parts['number']:\\n90:             # Numbers in parenthesis are \\\"series\\\" numbers\\n91:             end = match.end('number')\\n92:             if end < len(ddc) and ddc[end] == ')':\\n93:                 suffix += ' s'\\n94: \\n95:             # pad the integer part of the number\\n96:             number_parts = parts['number'].split('.')\\n97:             integer = number_parts[0]\\n98: \\n99:             # Copy decimal without losing precision\\n100:             decimal = '.' + number_parts[-1].strip() if len(number_parts) > 1 else ''\\n101: \\n102:             number = '%03d%s' % (int(integer), decimal)\\n103: \\n104:             # Discard catalog edition number\\n105:             # At least one classification number available\\n106:             if len(results) > 0:\\n107:                 # Check if number is without decimal component\\n108:                 if re.search(r'(^0?\\\\d{1,2}$)', parts['number']):\\n109:                     continue\\n110: \\n111:         # Handle [Fic] or [E]\\n112:         elif parts['fic']:\\n113:             number = '[%s]' % parts['fic'].title()\\n114:         else:\\n115:             continue\\n116: \\n117:         results.append(prefix + number + suffix)\\n118: \\n119:         # Include the non-j-prefixed form as well for correct sorting\\n120:         if prefix == 'j':\\n121:             results.append(number + suffix)\\n122: \\n123:     return results\\n124: \\n125: \\n126: def normalize_ddc_range(start: str, end: str) -> list[str | None]:\\n127:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n128:     Normalizes the pieces of a lucene (i.e. solr)-style range.\\n129:     E.g. ('23.23', '*')\\n130: \\n131:     >>> normalize_ddc_range('23.23', '*')\\n132:     ['023.23', '*']\\n133:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n134: \\n135:     ddc_range_norm: list[str | None] = []\\n136:     for ddc in start, end:\\n137:         if ddc == '*':\\n138:             ddc_range_norm.append('*')\\n139:         else:\\n140:             normed = normalize_ddc(ddc)\\n141:             if normed:\\n142:                 ddc_range_norm.append(normed[0])\\n143:             else:\\n144:                 ddc_range_norm.append(None)\\n145:     return ddc_range_norm\\n146: \\n147: \\n148: def normalize_ddc_prefix(prefix: str) -> str:\\n149:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n150:     Normalizes a DDC prefix to be used in searching. Integer prefixes are not modified\\n151: \\n152:     >>> normalize_ddc_prefix('1')\\n153:     '1'\\n154:     >>> normalize_ddc_prefix('1.1')\\n155:     '001.1'\\n156:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n157:     # 23.* should become 023*\\n158:     # {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016vgpthg3uo3Y3dDyBEY6qU","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Crash course Library of Congress Classification (LCC)\\n3: \\n4: Examples:\\n5:  - HB1951 .R64 1995\\n6:  - DP402.C8 O46 1995\\n7:  - CS879 .R3 1995\\n8:  - NC248.S22 A4 1992\\n9:  - TJ563 .P66 1998\\n10:  - PQ3919.2.M2866 C83 1994\\n11:  - NA2500 .H64 1995\\n12:  - DT423.E26 9th.ed. 2012\\n13:  - PZ73.S758345255 2011\\n14:  - PZ8.3.G276Lo 1971\\n15: \\n16: Has 3 parts:\\n17:  1. The class number: e.g. PQ3919.2 ; should match `[A-Z]{1,3}(\\\\\\\\d{1,4}(\\\\\\\\.\\\\\\\\d+)?)?`\\n18:  2. Cutter number(s): e.g. .M2866 C83\\n19:  3. Specification: Basically everything else, e.g. 2012, 9th.ed. 2012\\n20: \\n21: ### 1. The Class Number\\n22: The class number is what's used to determine the Library of Congress Subject. It has\\n23: a pretty well-defined structure, and should match `[A-Z]{1,3}(\\\\\\\\d{1,4}(\\\\\\\\.\\\\\\\\d+)?)?`\\n24: \\n25: For example:\\n26:  - QH -> Biology\\n27:  - QH426-470 -> Genetics\\n28: \\n29: _Note_: According to [1] (page 36), the first cutter is sometimes considered a part of\\n30: the class number. But this isn't discussed in [2], so it seems like it might not be\\n31: entirely well-defined.\\n32: \\n33: ### 2. Cutter Numbers\\n34: Cutter numbers are a somewhat phonetic hashing of a piece of \\\"extra\\\" information like\\n35: author last name, city, or whatever. Each character maps to a letter range, so for\\n36: example:\\n37:   - Idaho -> I33 -> I[d][a-d]\\n38:   - Campbell -> C36 -> C[a][m-o]\\n39: \\n40: For the full mapping of character to letter ranges, see [1] Appendix B1 (page 355).\\n41: \\n42: Because the number part of a cutter number maps to letters, even the numeral is sorted\\n43: lexicographically, so for example this is the correct sorting:\\n44:     [I2, I23, I3], **not** [I2, I3, I23]\\n45: \\n46: In essence they are sort of sorted as if they were decimal numbers.\\n47: \\n48: ### 3. Specification\\n49: \\n50: These aren't very well defined and could be just about anything. They usually include at\\n51: least the publication year of the edition, but might include edition numbers.\\n52: \\n53: ## Sorting\\n54: \\n55: To get _fully_ sortable LCCs, you likely need to use a multipart scheme (as described in\\n56: [2]). That's not really feasible for our Solr instance (especially since we store info\\n57: at the work level, which likely has multiple LCCs). The added complexity of that\\n58: approach is also not immediately worth it right now (but might be in the future).\\n59: \\n60: As a compromise, we make the class number and the first cutter sortable by making the\\n61: class number fixed-width. For example:\\n62:  - PZ73.S758345255 2011 -> PZ-0073.00000000.S758345255 2011\\n63:  - PZ8.3.G276Lo 1971    -> PZ-0008.30000000.G276Lo 1971\\n64: \\n65: This allows for range queries that could include the first cutter. It sorts incorrectly\\n66: if:\\n67:   - The decimal of the class number is longer than 8 digits (few such cases in OL db)\\n68:   - The sort is determined by information that appears after the first cutter\\n69:   - The first cutter is a \\\"double cutter\\\", e.g. B945.D4B65 199\\n70: \\n71: But it works for subject-related range queries, so we consider it sufficient.\\n72: \\n73: ## Further Reading\\n74: \\n75: - Wagner, Scott etal. \\\"A Comprehensive Approach to Algorithmic Machine Sorting of\\n76:     Library of Congress Call Numbers\\\" (2019) [1]\\n77: - LCTS/CCS-PCC Task Force on  Library of Congress Classification Training. \\\"Fundamentals\\n78:     of Library of Congress Classification\\\" (????) [2]\\n79: - LCC subjects as PDFs https://www.loc.gov/catdir/cpso/lcco/\\n80: - LCC subjects \\\"walkable\\\" tree http://id.loc.gov/authorities/classification.html\\n81: \\n82: ## References\\n83: \\n84: [1]: https://www.terkko.helsinki.fi/files/9666/classify_trnee_manual.pdf\\n85: [2]: https://ejournals.bc.edu/index.php/ital/article/download/11585/9839/\\n86: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n87: from __future__ import annotations\\n88: import re\\n89: from collections.abc import Iterable\\n90: \\n91: from openlibrary.utils.ddc import collapse_multiple_space\\n92: \\n93: # WARNING: Parts of this code have been translated into JS in\\n94: # LibraryExplorer/utils/lcc.js :(\\n95: # KEEP IN SYNC!\\n96: \\n97: LCC_PARTS_RE = re.compile(\\n98:     r'''\\n99:     ^\\n100:     # trailing dash only valid in \\\"sortable\\\" LCCs\\n101:     # Include W, even though technically part of NLM system\\n102:     (?P<letters>[A-HJ-NP-VWZ][A-Z-]{0,2})\\n103:     \\\\s?\\n104:     (?P<number>\\\\d{1,4}(\\\\.\\\\d+)?)?\\n105:     (?P<cutter1>\\\\s*\\\\.\\\\s*[^\\\\d\\\\s\\\\[]{1,3}\\\\d*\\\\S*)?\\n106:     (?P<rest>\\\\s.*)?\\n107:     $\\n108: ''',\\n109:     re.IGNORECASE | re.X,\\n110: )\\n111: \\n112: \\n113: def short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(lcc: str) -> str | None:\\n114:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n115:     See Sorting section of doc above\\n116:     :param str lcc: unformatted lcc\\n117:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n118:     m = LCC_PARTS_RE.match(clean_raw_lcc(lcc))\\n119:     if not m:\\n120:         return None\\n121: \\n122:     parts = m.groupdict()\\n123:     parts['letters'] = parts['letters'].upper().ljust(3, '-')\\n124:     parts['number'] = float(parts['number'] or 0)\\n125:     parts['cutter1'] = '.' + parts['cutter1'].lstrip(' .') if parts['cutter1'] else ''\\n126:     parts['rest'] = ' ' + parts['rest'].strip() if parts['rest'] else ''\\n127: \\n128:     # There will often be a CPB Box No (whatever that is) in the LCC field;\\n129:     # E.g. \\\"CPB Box no. 1516 vol. 17\\\"\\n130:     # Although this might be useful to search by, it's not really an LCC,\\n131:     # so considering it invalid here.\\n132:     if parts['letters'] == 'CPB':\\n133:         return None\\n134: \\n135:     return '%(letters)s%(number)013.8f%(cutter1)s%(rest)s' % parts\\n136: \\n137: \\n138: def sortable_lcc_to_short_lcc(lcc: str) -> str:\\n139:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n140:     As close to the inverse of make_sortable_lcc as possible\\n141:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n142:     m = LCC_PARTS_RE.match(lcc)\\n143:     assert m, f'Unable to parse LCC \\\"{lcc}\\\"'\\n144:     parts = m.groupdict()\\n145:     parts['letters'] = parts['letters'].strip('-')\\n146:     parts['number'] = parts['number'].strip('0').strip('.')  # Need to do in order!\\n147:     parts['cutter1'] = parts['cutter1'].strip(' ') if parts['cutter1'] else ''\\n148:     parts['rest'] = ' ' + parts['rest'].strip() if parts['rest'] else ''\\n149: \\n150:     return '%(letters)s%(number)s%(cutter1)s%(rest)s' % parts\\n151: \\n152: \\n153: def clean_raw_lcc(raw_lcc: str) -> str:\\n154:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n155:     Remove noise in lcc before matching to LCC_PARTS_RE\\n156:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n157:     lcc = collapse_multiple_space(raw_lcc.replace('\\\\\\\\', ' ').strip(' '))\\n158:     if (lcc.startswith('[') and lcc.endswith(']')) or (\\n159:         lcc.startswith('(') and lcc.endswith(')')\\n160:     ):\\n161:         lcc = lcc[1:-1]\\n162:     return lcc\\n163: \\n164: \\n165: def normalize_lcc_prefix(prefix: str) -> str | None:\\n166:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n167:     :param str prefix: An LCC prefix\\n168:     :return: Prefix transformed to be a prefix for sortable LCC\\n169: \\n170:     >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123')\\n171:     'A--0123'\\n172:     >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123.')\\n173:     'A--0123'\\n174:     >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123.0')\\n175:     'A--0123.0'\\n176:     >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123.C')\\n177:     'A--0123.00000000.C'\\n178:     >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123.C0')\\n179:     'A--0123.00000000.C0'\\n180:     >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('E--')\\n181:     'E--'\\n182:     >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('PN-')\\n183:     'PN-'\\n184:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n185:     if re.match(r'^[A-Z]+$', prefix, re.I):\\n186:         return prefix\\n187:     else:\\n188:         lcc_norm = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(prefix.rstrip('.'))\\n189:         if lcc_norm:\\n190:             result = lcc_norm.rstrip('0')\\n191:             if '.' in prefix and prefix.endswith('0'):\\n192: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Crash course Library of Congress Classification (LCC)\\n3: \\n4: Examples:\\n5:  - HB1951 .R64 1995\\n6:  - DP402.C8 O46 1995\\n7:  - CS879 .R3 1995\\n8:  - NC248.S22 A4 1992\\n9:  - TJ563 .P66 1998\\n10:  - PQ3919.2.M2866 C83 1994\\n11:  - NA2500 .H64 1995\\n12:  - DT423.E26 9th.ed. 2012\\n13:  - PZ73.S758345255 2011\\n14:  - PZ8.3.G276Lo 1971\\n15: \\n16: Has 3 parts:\\n17:  1. The class number: e.g. PQ3919.2 ; should match `[A-Z]{1,3}(\\\\\\\\d{1,4}(\\\\\\\\.\\\\\\\\d+)?)?`\\n18:  2. Cutter number(s): e.g. .M2866 C83\\n19:  3. Specification: Basically everything else, e.g. 2012, 9th.ed. 2012\\n20: \\n21: ### 1. The Class Number\\n22: The class number is what's used to determine the Library of Congress Subject. It has\\n23: a pretty well-defined structure, and should match `[A-Z]{1,3}(\\\\\\\\d{1,4}(\\\\\\\\.\\\\\\\\d+)?)?`\\n24: \\n25: For example:\\n26:  - QH -> Biology\\n27:  - QH426-470 -> Genetics\\n28: \\n29: _Note_: According to [1] (page 36), the first cutter is sometimes considered a part of\\n30: the class number. But this isn't discussed in [2], so it seems like it might not be\\n31: entirely well-defined.\\n32: \\n33: ### 2. Cutter Numbers\\n34: Cutter numbers are a somewhat phonetic hashing of a piece of \\\"extra\\\" information like\\n35: author last name, city, or whatever. Each character maps to a letter range, so for\\n36: example:\\n37:   - Idaho -> I33 -> I[d][a-d]\\n38:   - Campbell -> C36 -> C[a][m-o]\\n39: \\n40: For the full mapping of character to letter ranges, see [1] Appendix B1 (page 355).\\n41: \\n42: Because the number part of a cutter number maps to letters, even the numeral is sorted\\n43: lexicographically, so for example this is the correct sorting:\\n44:     [I2, I23, I3], **not** [I2, I3, I23]\\n45: \\n46: In essence they are sort of sorted as if they were decimal numbers.\\n47: \\n48: ### 3. Specification\\n49: \\n50: These aren't very well defined and could be just about anything. They usually include at\\n51: least the publication year of the edition, but might include edition numbers.\\n52: \\n53: ## Sorting\\n54: \\n55: To get _fully_ sortable LCCs, you likely need to use a multipart scheme (as described in\\n56: [2]). That's not really feasible for our Solr instance (especially since we store info\\n57: at the work level, which likely has multiple LCCs). The added complexity of that\\n58: approach is also not immediately worth it right now (but might be in the future).\\n59: \\n60: As a compromise, we make the class number and the first cutter sortable by making the\\n61: class number fixed-width. For example:\\n62:  - PZ73.S758345255 2011 -> PZ-0073.00000000.S758345255 2011\\n63:  - PZ8.3.G276Lo 1971    -> PZ-0008.30000000.G276Lo 1971\\n64: \\n65: This allows for range queries that could include the first cutter. It sorts incorrectly\\n66: if:\\n67:   - The decimal of the class number is longer than 8 digits (few such cases in OL db)\\n68:   - The sort is determined by information that appears after the first cutter\\n69:   - The first cutter is a \\\"double cutter\\\", e.g. B945.D4B65 199\\n70: \\n71: But it works for subject-related range queries, so we consider it sufficient.\\n72: \\n73: ## Further Reading\\n74: \\n75: - Wagner, Scott etal. \\\"A Comprehensive Approach to Algorithmic Machine Sorting of\\n76:     Library of Congress Call Numbers\\\" (2019) [1]\\n77: - LCTS/CCS-PCC Task Force on  Library of Congress Classification Training. \\\"Fundamentals\\n78:     of Library of Congress Classification\\\" (????) [2]\\n79: - LCC subjects as PDFs https://www.loc.gov/catdir/cpso/lcco/\\n80: - LCC subjects \\\"walkable\\\" tree http://id.loc.gov/authorities/classification.html\\n81: \\n82: ## References\\n83: \\n84: [1]: https://www.terkko.helsinki.fi/files/9666/classify_trnee_manual.pdf\\n85: [2]: https://ejournals.bc.edu/index.php/ital/article/download/11585/9839/\\n86: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n87: from __future__ If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\n            luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\n        else:\n            parent.children = new_children\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Not supported for generic class Item\")\n\n\ndef luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\n\n    Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\n    modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\n\n    :param item: Node to traverse\n    :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\n    \"\"\"\n    parents = _parents or []\n    yield item, parents\n    new_parents = [*parents, item]\n    for child in item.children:\n        yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\n\n\ndef escape_unknown_fields(\n    query: str,\n    is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\n    lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar   blah:bar baz:boo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar   blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'hi'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('(title:foo) OR (blah:bah)', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    '(title:foo) OR (blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bah)'\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n    # Note we use the string of the tree, because it strips spaces\n    # like: \"title : foo\" -> \"title:foo\"\n    escaped_query = str(tree)\n    offset = 0\n    for sf, _ in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(\n            sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name\n        ):\n            field = sf.name + r'\\:'\n            if hasattr(sf, 'head'):\n                # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;\n                # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing\n                # together\n                field = sf.head + field\n\n            # We will be moving left to right, so we need to adjust the offset\n            # to account for the characters we have already replaced\n            escaped_query = (\n                escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]\n                + field\n                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 1 + offset :]\n            )\n fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = re.sub(r'[\\[\\]\\(\\)\\{\\}:\"\\-+?~^/\\\\,]', r'\\\\\\g<0>', escaped)\n    # Remove boolean operators by making them lowercase\n    escaped = re.sub(r'AND|OR|NOT', lambda _1: _1.group(0).lower(), escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n    'title:(foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n    'title:(YES and)'\n    \"\"\"\n    result = ''\n    if escaped:\n        result += f' {op} '.join(\n            f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\n        )\n    if unescaped:\n        if result:\n            result += f' {op} '\n        result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n    return result\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7099,"cache_read_input_tokens":57007,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":7099},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a398a29e-e698-4f91-b5cd-2af9bc40715a","uuid":"3752c8cc-bc4a-46b7-9a20-22ab664a1227"}

Allow custom randoms; so anything random_* is allowed\n                return sort if ' ' in sort else f'{sort} asc'\n            else:\n                solr_sort = self.sorts[sort]\n                return solr_sort() if callable(solr_sort) else solr_sort\n\n        return ','.join(\n            process_individual_sort(s.strip()) for s in user_sort.split(',')\n        )\n\n    def process_user_query(self, q_param: str) -> str:\n        if q_param == '*:*':\n            # Otherwise\n                    # `key:/works/OL1W` is interpreted as a regex.\n                    q_param.strip()\n                    .replace('/', '\\\\/')\n                    # Also escape unexposed lucene features\n                    .replace('?', '\\\\?')\n                    .replace('~', '\\\\~')\n                    .replace('-', '\\\\-')\n                ),\n                self.is_search_field,\n                lower=True,\n            )\n            q_tree = luqum_parser(q_param)\n        except ParseError:\n            # This isn't a syntactically valid lucene query\n            logger.warning(\"Invalid lucene query\", exc_info=True)\n            # Escape everything we can\n            q_tree = luqum_parser(fully_escape_query(q_param))\n\n        q_tree = self.transform_user_query(q_param, q_tree)\n        return str(q_tree)\n\n    def transform_user_query(\n        self,\n        user_query: str,\n        q_tree: luqum.tree.Item,\n    ) -> luqum.tree.Item:\n        return q_tree\n\n    def build_q_from_params(self, params: dict) -> Optional[str]:\n        return None\n\n    def q_to_solr_params(self, q: str, solr_fields: set[str]) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n        return [('q', q)]\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":221,"cache_read_input_tokens":69923,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":221},"output_tokens":4,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a398a29e-e698-4f91-b5cd-2af9bc40715a","uuid":"fe301627-99d7-4e91-9029-269849b00126"}

user_query: str, q_tree: luqum.tree.Item\n    ) -> luqum.tree.Item:\n        has_search_fields = False\n        for node, parents in luqum_traverse(q_tree):\n            if isinstance(node, luqum.tree.SearchField):\n                has_search_fields = True\n                if node.name.lower() in self.field_name_map:\n                    node.name = self.field_name_map[node.name.lower()]\n                if node.name == 'isbn':\n                    isbn_transform(node)\n                if node.name in ('lcc', 'lcc_sort'):\n                    lcc_transform(node)\n                if node.name in ('dcc', 'dcc_sort'):\n                    ddc_transform(node)\n                if node.name == 'ia_collection_s':\n                    ia_collection_s_transform(node)\n\n        if not has_search_fields:\n            # If there are no search fields, maybe we want just an isbn?\n            isbn = normalize_isbn(user_query)\n            if isbn and len(isbn) in (10, 13):\n                q_tree = luqum_parser(f'isbn:({isbn})')\n\n        return q_tree\n\n    def build_q_from_params(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n        q_list = []\n        if 'author' in params:\n            v = params['author'].strip()\n            m = re_author_key.search(v)\n            if m:\n                q_list.append(f\"author_key:({m.group(1)})\")\n            else:\n                v = fully_escape_query(v)\n                q_list.append(f\"(author_name:({v}) OR author_alternative_name:({v}))\")\n\n        check_params = {\n            'title',\n            'publisher',\n            'oclc',\n            'lccn',\n            'contributor',\n            'subject',\n            'place',\n            'person',\n            'time',\n            'author_key',\n        }\n        # support web.input fields being either a list or string\n        # when default values used\n        q_list += [\n            f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(val)})'\n            for k in (check_params & set(params))\n            for val in (params[k] if isinstance(params[k], list) else [params[k]])\n        ]\n\n        if params.get('isbn'):\n            q_list.append(\n                'isbn:(%s)' % (normalize_isbn(params['isbn']) or params['isbn'])\n            )\n\n        return ' AND '.join(q_list)\n\n    def q_to_solr_params(self, q: str, solr_fields: set[str]) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n        params: list[tuple[str, str]] = []\n\n        # We need to parse the tree so that it gets transformed using the\n        # special OL query parsing rules (different from default solr!)\n        # See luqum_parser for details.\n        work_q_tree = luqum_parser(q)\n        params.append(('workQuery', str(work_q_tree)))\n\n        # This full work query uses solr-specific syntax to add extra parameters\n        # to the way the search is processed. We are using the edismax parser.\n        # See https://solr.apache.org/guide/8_11/the-extended-dismax-query-parser.html\n        # This is somewhat synonymous to setting defType=edismax in the\n        # query, but much more flexible. We wouldn't be able to do our\n        # complicated parent/child queries with defType!\n\n        full_work_query = '({{!edismax q.op=\"AND\" qf=\"{qf}\" bf=\"{bf}\" v={v}}})'.format(\n            # qf: the fields to query un-prefixed parts of the query.\n            # e.g. 'harry potter' becomes\n            # 'text:(harry potter) OR alternative_title:(harry potter)^20 OR ...'\n            qf='text alternative_title^20 author_name^20',\n            # bf (boost factor): boost results based on the value of this\n            # field. I.e. results with more editions get boosted, upto a\n            # max of 100, after which we don't see it as good signal of\n            # quality.\n            bf='min(100,edition_count)',\n            # v: the query to process with the edismax query parser. Note\n            # we are using a solr variable here; this reads the url parameter\n            # arbitrarily called workQuery.\n            v='$workQuery',\n        )\n\n        ed_q = None\n        editions_fq = []\n        if has_solr_editions_enabled() and 'editions:[subquery]' in solr_fields:\n            WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD = {\n                # Internals\n                'edition_key': 'key',\n                'text': 'text',\n                # Display data\n                'title': 'title',\n                'title_suggest': 'title_suggest',\n                'subtitle': 'subtitle',\n                'alternative_title': 'title',\n                'alternative_subtitle': 'subtitle',\n                'cover_i': 'cover_i',\n                # Misc useful data\n                'language': 'language',\n                'publisher': 'publisher',\n                'publisher_facet': 'publisher_facet',\n                'publish_date': 'publish_date',\n                'publish_year': 'publish_year',\n                # Identifiers\n                'isbn': 'isbn',\n                # 'id_*': 'id_*', # Handled manually for now to match any id field\n                'ebook_access': 'ebook_access',\n                # IA\n                'has_fulltext': 'has_fulltext',\n                'ia': 'ia',\n                'ia_collection': 'ia_collection',\n                'ia_box_id': 'ia_box_id',\n                'public_scan_b': 'public_scan_b',\n            }\n\n            def convert_work_field_to_edition_field(field: str) -> Optional[str]:\n                \"\"\"\n                Convert a SearchField name (eg 'title') to the correct fieldname\n                for use in an edition query.\n\n                If no conversion is possible, return None.\n                \"\"\"\n                if field in WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD:\n                    return WORK_FIELD_TO_ED_FIELD[field]\n                elif field.startswith('id_'):\n                    return field\n                elif field in self.all_fields or field in self.facet_fields:\n                    return None\n                else:\n                    raise ValueError(f'Unknown field: {field}')\n\n            def convert_work_query_to_edition_query(work_query: str) -> str:\n                \"\"\"\n                Convert a work query to an edition query. Mainly involves removing\n                invalid fields, or renaming fields as necessary.\n                \"\"\"\n                q_tree = luqum_parser(work_query)\n\n                for node, parents in luqum_traverse(q_tree):\n                    if isinstance(node, luqum.tree.SearchField) and node.name != '*':\n                        new_name = convert_work_field_to_edition_field(node.name)\n                        if new_name:\n                            parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n                            # Prefixing with + makes the field mandatory\n                            if isinstance(\n                                parent, (luqum.tree.Not, luqum.tree.Prohibit)\n                            ):\n                                node.name = new_name\n                            else:\n                                node.name = f'+{new_name}'\n                        else:\n                            try:\n                                luqum_remove_child(node, parents)\n                            except EmptyTreeError:\n                                # Deleted the whole tree!               # boosting.\n                v=ed_q.replace('\"', '\\\\\"') or '*:*',\n                # bq (boost query): Boost which edition is promoted to the top\n                bq=' '.join(\n 'ebook_access:printdisabled^2',\n                        'cover_i:*^2',\n                    )\n                ),\n            )\n\n        if ed_q or len(editions_fq) > 1:\n            # The full_ed_query))\n            else:\n                params.append(('edQuery', '*:*'))\n            q = (\n                f'+{full_work_query} '\n                # This is using the special parent query syntax to, on top of\n                # the user's `full_work_query`, also only find works which have\n                # editions matching the edition query.\n                # Also include edition-less works (i.e. edition_count:0)\n                '+('\n                '_query_:\"{!parent which=type:work v=$edQuery filters=$editions.fq}\" '\n                'OR edition_count:0'\n                ')'\n            )\n            params.append(('q', q))\n            edition_fields = {\n                f.split('.', 1)[1] for f in solr_fields if f.startswith('editions.')\n            }\n            if not edition_fields:\n                edition_fields = solr_fields - {'editions:[subquery]'}\n            # The elements in _this_ edition query will match but not affect\n            # whether the work appears in search results\n            params.append(\n                (\n                    'editions.q',\n                    # Here we use the special terms parser to only filter the\n                    # editions for a given, already matching work '_root_' node.\n                    f'({{!terms f=_root_ v=$row.key}}) AND {full_ed_query}',\n                )\n            )\n            params.append(('editions.rows', '1'))\n            params.append(('editions.fl', ','.join(edition_fields)))\n        else:\n            params.append(('q', full_work_query))\n\n        return params\n\n\ndef lcc_transform(sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):\n    # e.g. lcc:[NC1 TO NC1000] to lcc:[NC-0001.00000000 TO NC-1000.00000000]\n    # for proper range search\n    val = sf.children[0]\n    if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):\n        normed_range = normalize_lcc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)\n        if normed_range:\n            val.low.value, val.high.value = normed_range\n    elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word):\n        if '*' in val.value and not val.value.startswith('*'):\n            # Marshals human repr into solr repr\n            # lcc:A720* should become A--0720*\n            parts = val.value.split('*', 1)\n            lcc_prefix = normalize_lcc_prefix(parts[0])\n            val.value = (lcc_prefix or parts[0]) + '*' + parts[1]\n        else:\n            normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(val.value.strip('\"'))\n            if normed:\n                val.value = normed\n    elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Phrase):\n        normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(val.value.strip('\"'))\n        if normed:\n            val.value = f'\"{normed}\"'\n    elif (\n        isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Group)\n        and isinstance(val.expr, luqum.tree.UnknownOperation)\n        and all(isinstance(c, luqum.tree.Word) for c in val.expr.children)\n    ):\n        # treat it as a string\n        normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(str(val.expr))\n        if normed:\n            if ' ' in normed:\n                sf.expr = luqum.tree.Phrase(f'\"{normed}\"')\n            else:\n sf.children[0]\n    if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):\n        normed_range = normalize_ddc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)\n        val.low.value = normed_range[0] or val.low\n        val.high.value = normed_range[1] or val.high\n    elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word) and val.value.endswith('*'):\n        return normalize_ddc_prefix(val.value[:-1]) + '*'\n    elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word) or isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Phrase):\n        normed = normalize_ddc(val.value.strip('\"'))\n        if normed:\n            val.value = normed\n    else:\n        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected ddc SearchField value type: {type(val)}\")\n\n\ndef isbn_transform(sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):\n    field_val = sf.children[0]\n    if isinstance(field_val, luqum.tree.Word) and '*' not in field_val.value:\n        isbn = normalize_isbn(field_val.value)\n        if isbn:\n            field_val.value = isbn\n    else:\n        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected isbn SearchField value type: {type(field_val)}\")\n\n\ndef ia_collection_s_transform(sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):\n    \"\"\"\n    Because this field is not a multi-valued field in solr, but a simple     params.append(('workQuery', str(work_q_tree)))\n\n        # This full work query uses solr-specific syntax to add extra parameters\n        # to the way the search is processed. We wouldn't be able to do our\n        # complicated parent/child queries with defType!\n\n        full_work_query = '({{!edismax q.op=\"AND\" qf=\"{qf}\" bf=\"{bf}\" v={v}}})'.format(\n            # qf: the fields to query un-prefixed parts of the query.\n            # e.g. 'harry potter' becomes\n            # 'text:(harry potter) OR alternative_title:(harry potter)^20 OR ...'\n            qf='text alternative_title^20 author_name^20',\n            # bf (boost factor): boost results based on the value of this\n            # field. I.e. results with more editions get boosted, upto a\n            # max of 100, after which we don't see it as good signal of\n            # quality.\n            bf='min(100,edition_count)',\n            # v: the query to process with the edismax query parser. Prefix\n        (?P<prestar>\\*)?  # Should be suffix\n        (?P<neg>-)?  # Old standard; no longer used\n        (?P<j>j)?  # Juvenile prefix\n        C?  # Canadian CIP\n\n        # The number\n        (?P<number>\\d{1,3}(\\.+\\s?\\d+)?)\n\n        # Suffix\n        (?P<poststar>\\*?)\n        (?P<s>\\s?s)?  # Series suffix\n        (?P<B>\\s?\\[?B\\]?)?  # Biographical\n        (?P<ninetwo>\\s(092|920|92))?  # No clue; shouldn't be its own DDC though\n    )\n    |\n    (\\[?(?P<fic>Fic|E)\\.?\\]?)\n''',\n    re.IGNORECASE | re.X,\n)\n\n\ndef collapse_multiple_space(s: str) -> str:\n    return MULTIPLE_SPACES_RE.sub(' ', s)\n\n\nVALID_CHARS = set(printable) - set(\"/'′',\")\n\n\ndef normalize_ddc(ddc: str) -> list[str]:\n    ddc = ''.join(\n        char for char in collapse_multiple_space(ddc.strip()) if char in VALID_CHARS\n    )\n\n    results: list[str] = []\n    for match in DDC_RE.finditer(ddc):\n        parts = match.groupdict()\n        prefix = ''\n        suffix = ''\n\n        # DDCs should start at word boundaries\n        start = match.start()\n        if start > 0 and re.search(r'\\b', ddc[start - 1]):\n            continue\n        # And end at them\n        end = match.end()\n        if end < len(ddc) and re.search(r'\\b', ddc[end]):\n            continue\n\n        # Some old standard which isn't used anymore; might need to filter these\n        # out, but they should sort OK so let's keep them.\n        if parts['neg']:\n            prefix += '-'\n        # Juvenile prefix\n        if parts['j']:\n            prefix += 'j'\n\n        # Star should be at end\n        if parts['prestar'] or parts['poststar']:\n            suffix = '*'\n        # Series suffix\n        if parts['s']:\n            suffix += ' s'\n        # Biographical\n        if parts['B']:\n            suffix += ' B'\n        # Not at all sure\n        if parts['ninetwo']:\n            suffix += parts['ninetwo']\n\n        # And now the actual number!\n        if parts['number']:\n            # Numbers in parenthesis are \"series\" numbers\n            end = match.end('number')\n            if end < len(ddc) and ddc[end] == ')':\n                suffix += ' s'\n\n            # pad the integer part of the number\n            number_parts = parts['number'].split('.')\n            integer = number_parts[0]\n\n            # Copy decimal without losing precision\n            decimal = '.' + number_parts[-1].strip() if len(number_parts) > 1 else ''\n\n            number = '%03d%s' % (int(integer), decimal)\n\n            # Discard catalog edition number\n            # At least one classification number available\n            if len(results) > 0:\n                # Check if number is without decimal component\n                if re.search(r'(^0?\\d{1,2}$)', parts['number']):\n                    continue\n\n        # Handle [Fic] or [E]\n        elif parts['fic']:\n            number = '[%s]' % parts['fic'].title()\n        else:\n            continue\n\n        results.append(prefix + number + suffix)\n\n        # Include the non-j-prefixed form as well for correct sorting\n        if prefix == 'j':\n            results.append(number + suffix)\n\n    return results\n\n\ndef normalize_ddc_range(start: str, end: str) -> list[str | None]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Normalizes the pieces of a lucene (i.e. solr)-style range.\n    E.g. ('23.23', '*')\n\n    >>> normalize_ddc_range('23.23', '*')\n    ['023.23', '*']\n    \"\"\"\n\n    ddc_range_norm: list[str | None] = []\n    for ddc in start, end:\n        if ddc == '*':\n            ddc_range_norm.append('*')\n        else:\n            normed = normalize_ddc(ddc)\n            if normed:\n                ddc_range_norm.append(normed[0])\n            else:\n                ddc_range_norm.append(None)\n    return ddc_range_norm\n\n\ndef normalize_ddc_prefix(prefix: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Normalizes a DDC prefix to be used in searching. Integer prefixes are not modified\n\n    >>> normalize_ddc_prefix('1')\n    '1'\n    >>> normalize_ddc_prefix('1.1')\n    '001.1'\n    \"\"\"\n    # 23.* should become 023*\n    # 23.45* should become 023.45*\n    if '.' in prefix:\n        normed = normalize_ddc(prefix)\n        return normed[0] if normed else prefix\n    # 0* should stay as is\n    # 23* should stay as is\n    # j* should stay as is\n    else:\n        return prefix\n\n\ndef choose_sorting_ddc(normalized_ddcs: Iterable[str]) -> str:\n    # Prefer unprefixed DDCs (so they sort correctly)\n    preferred_ddcs = [ddc for ddc in normalized_ddcs if ddc[0] in '0123456789']\n    # Choose longest; theoretically most precise?\n    return sorted(preferred_ddcs or normalized_ddcs, key=len, reverse=True)[0]\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":911,"cache_read_input_tokens":77478,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":911},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a398a29e-e698-4f91-b5cd-2af9bc40715a","uuid":"bd80ec3f-85c0-4027-9474-3ead0c42ec3e"}

Suffix\n        (?P<poststar>\\*?)\n        (?P<s>\\s?s)?  # Series suffix\n        (?P<B>\\s?\\[?B\\]?)?  # Biographical\n        (?P<ninetwo>\\s(092|920|92))?  # No clue; shouldn't be its own DDC though\n    )\n    |\n    (\\[?(?P<fic>Fic|E)\\.?\\]?)\n''',\n    re.IGNORECASE | re.X,\n)\n\n\ndef collapse_multiple_space(s: str) -> str:\n    return MULTIPLE_SPACES_RE.sub(' ', s)\n\n\nVALID_CHARS = set(printable) - set(\"/'′',\")\n\n\ndef normalize_ddc(ddc: str) -> list[str]:\n    ddc = ''.join(\n        char for char in collapse_multiple_space(ddc.strip()) if char in VALID_CHARS\n    )\n\n    results: list[str] = []\n    for match in DDC_RE.finditer(ddc):\n        parts = match.groupdict()\n        prefix = ''\n        suffix = ''\n\n        # DDCs should start at word boundaries\n        start = match.start()\n        if start > 0 and re.search(r'\\b', ddc[start - 1]):\n            continue\n        # And end at them\n        end = match.end()\n        if end < len(ddc) and re.search(r'\\b', ddc[end]):\n            continue\n\n        # Some old standard which isn't used anymore; might need to filter these\n        # out, but they should sort OK so let's keep them.\n        if parts['neg']:\n            prefix += '-'\n        # Juvenile prefix\n        if parts['j']:\n            prefix += 'j'\n\n        # Star should be at end\n        if parts['prestar'] or parts['poststar']:\n            suffix = '*'\n        # Series suffix\n        if parts['s']:\n            suffix += ' s'\n        # Biographical\n        if parts['B']:\n            suffix += ' B'\n        # Not at all sure\n        if parts['ninetwo']:\n            suffix += parts['ninetwo']\n\n        # And now the actual number!\n        if parts['number']:\n            # Numbers in parenthesis are \"series\" numbers\n            end = match.end('number')\n            if end < len(ddc) and ddc[end] == ')':\n                suffix += ' s'\n\n            # pad the integer part of the number\n            number_parts = parts['number'].split('.')\n            integer = number_parts[0]\n\n            # Copy decimal without losing precision\n            decimal = '.' + number_parts[-1].strip() if len(number_parts) > 1 else ''\n\n            number = '%03d%s' % (int(integer), decimal)\n\n            # Discard catalog edition number\n            # At least one classification number available\n            if len(results) > 0:\n                # Check if number is without decimal component\n                if re.search(r'(^0?\\d{1,2}$)', parts['number']):\n                    continue\n\n        # Handle [Fic] or [E]\n        elif parts['fic']:\n            number = '[%s]' % parts['fic'].title()\n        else:\n            continue\n\n        results.append(prefix + number + suffix)\n\n        # Include the non-j-prefixed form as well for correct sorting\n        if prefix == 'j':\n            results.append(number + suffix)\n\n    return results\n\n\ndef normalize_ddc_range(start: str, end: str) -> list[str | None]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Normalizes the pieces of a lucene (i.e. solr)-style range.\n    E.g. ('23.23', '*')\n\n    >>> normalize_ddc_range('23.23', '*')\n    ['023.23', '*']\n    \"\"\"\n\n    ddc_range_norm: list[str | None] = []\n    for ddc in start, end:\n        if ddc == '*':\n            ddc_range_norm.append('*')\n        else:\n            normed = normalize_ddc(ddc)\n            if normed:\n                ddc_range_norm.append(normed[0])\n            else:\n                ddc_range_norm.append(None)\n    return ddc_range_norm\n\n\ndef normalize_ddc_prefix(prefix: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Normalizes a DDC prefix to be used in searching. Integer prefixes are not modified\n\n    >>> normalize_ddc_prefix('1')\n    '1'\n    >>> normalize_ddc_prefix('1.1')\n    '001.1'\n    \"\"\"\n    # 23.* should become 023*\n    # 23.45* should become 023.45*\n    if '.' in prefix:\n        normed = normalize_ddc(prefix)\n        return normed[0] if normed else prefix\n    # 0* should stay as is\n    # 23* should stay as is\n    # j* should stay as is\n    else:\n        return prefix\n\n\ndef choose_sorting_ddc(normalized_ddcs: Iterable[str]) -> str:\n    # Prefer unprefixed DDCs (so they sort correctly)\n    preferred_ddcs = [ddc for ddc in normalized_ddcs if ddc[0] in '0123456789']\n    # Choose longest; theoretically most precise?\n    return sorted(preferred_ddcs or normalized_ddcs, key=len, reverse=True)[0]\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_019a9t2MvCbkRX7kCdMKCAgj","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DUvgvxSHB7NVNbVVcfL1jj","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/openlibrary/utils/lcc.py","content":"\"\"\"\nCrash course Library of Congress Classification (LCC)\n\nExamples:\n - HB1951 .R64 1995\n - DP402.C8 O46 1995\n - CS879 .R3 1995\n - NC248.S22 A4 1992\n - TJ563 .P66 1998\n - PQ3919.2.M2866 C83 1994\n - NA2500 .H64 1995\n - DT423.E26 9th.ed. 2012\n - PZ73.S758345255 2011\n - PZ8.3.G276Lo 1971\n\nHas 3 parts:\n 1. The class number: e.g. PQ3919.2 ; should match `[A-Z]{1,3}(\\\\\\\\d{1,4}(\\\\\\\\.\\\\\\\\d+)?)?`\n 2. Cutter number(s): e.g. .M2866 C83\n 3. Specification: Basically everything else, e.g. 2012, 9th.ed. 2012\n\n### 1. The Class Number\nThe class number is what's used to determine the Library of Congress Subject. It has\na pretty well-defined structure, and should match `[A-Z]{1,3}(\\\\\\\\d{1,4}(\\\\\\\\.\\\\\\\\d+)?)?`\n\nFor example:\n - QH -> Biology\n - QH426-470 -> Genetics\n\n_Note_: According to [1] (page 36), the first cutter is sometimes considered a part of\nthe class number. But this isn't discussed in [2], so it seems like it might not be\nentirely well-defined.\n\n### 2. Cutter Numbers\nCutter numbers are a somewhat phonetic hashing of a piece of \"extra\" information like\nauthor last name, city, or whatever. Each character maps to a letter range, so for\nexample:\n  - Idaho -> I33 -> I[d][a-d]\n  - Campbell -> C36 -> C[a][m-o]\n\nFor the full mapping of character to letter ranges, see [1] Appendix B1 (page 355).\n\nBecause the number part of a cutter number maps to letters, even the numeral is sorted\nlexicographically, so for example this is the correct sorting:\n    [I2, I23, I3], **not** [I2, I3, I23]\n\nIn essence they are sort of sorted as if they were decimal numbers.\n\n### 3. Specification\n\nThese aren't very well defined and could be just about anything. They usually include at\nleast the publication year of the edition, but might include edition numbers.\n\n## Sorting\n\nTo get _fully_ sortable LCCs, you likely need to use a multipart scheme (as described in\n[2]). That's not really feasible for our Solr instance (especially since we store info\nat the work level, which likely has multiple LCCs). The added complexity of that\napproach is also not immediately worth it right now (but might be in the future).\n\nAs a compromise, we make the class number and the first cutter sortable by making the\nclass number fixed-width. For example:\n - PZ73.S758345255 2011 -> PZ-0073.00000000.S758345255 2011\n - PZ8.3.G276Lo 1971    -> PZ-0008.30000000.G276Lo 1971\n\nThis allows for range queries that could include the first cutter. It sorts incorrectly\nif:\n   - The decimal of the class number is longer than 8 digits (few such cases in OL db)\n   - The sort is determined by information that appears after the first cutter\n   - The first cutter is a \"double cutter\", e.g. B945.D4B65 199\n\nBut it works for subject-related range queries, so we consider it sufficient.\n\n## Further Reading\n\n- Wagner, Scott etal. \"A Comprehensive Approach to Algorithmic Machine Sorting of\n    Library of Congress Call Numbers\" (2019) [1]\n- LCTS/CCS-PCC Task Force on  Library of Congress Classification Training. \"Fundamentals\n    of Library of Congress Classification\" (????) [2]\n- LCC subjects as PDFs https://www.loc.gov/catdir/cpso/lcco/\n- LCC subjects \"walkable\" tree http://id.loc.gov/authorities/classification.html\n\n## References\n\n[1]: https://www.terkko.helsinki.fi/files/9666/classify_trnee_manual.pdf\n[2]: https://ejournals.bc.edu/index.php/ital/article/download/11585/9839/\n\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport re\nfrom collections.abc import Iterable\n\nfrom openlibrary.utils.ddc import collapse_multiple_space\n\n# WARNING: Parts of this code have been translated into JS in\n# LibraryExplorer/utils/lcc.js :(\n# KEEP IN SYNC!\n\nLCC_PARTS_RE = re.compile(\n    r'''\n    ^\n    # trailing dash only valid in \"sortable\" LCCs\n    # Include W, even though technically part of NLM system\n    (?P<letters>[A-HJ-NP-VWZ][A-Z-]{0,2})\n    \\s?\n    (?P<number>\\d{1,4}(\\.\\d+)?)?\n    (?P<cutter1>\\s*\\.\\s*[^\\d\\s\\[]{1,3}\\d*\\S*)?\n    (?P<rest>\\s.*)?\n    $\n''',\n    re.IGNORECASE | re.X,\n)\n\n\ndef short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(lcc: str) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    See Sorting section of doc above\n    :param str lcc: unformatted lcc\n    \"\"\"\n    m = LCC_PARTS_RE.match(clean_raw_lcc(lcc))\n    if not m:\n        return None\n\n    parts = m.groupdict()\n    parts['letters'] = parts['letters'].upper().ljust(3, '-')\n    parts['number'] = float(parts['number'] or 0)\n    parts['cutter1'] = '.' + parts['cutter1'].lstrip(' .') if parts['cutter1'] else ''\n    parts['rest'] = ' ' + parts['rest'].strip() if parts['rest'] else ''\n\n    # There will often be a CPB Box No (whatever that is) in the LCC field;\n    # E.g. \"CPB Box no. 1516 vol. 17\"\n    # Although this might be useful to search by, it's not really an LCC,\n    # so considering it invalid here.\n    if parts['letters'] == 'CPB':\n        return None\n\n    return '%(letters)s%(number)013.8f%(cutter1)s%(rest)s' % parts\n\n\ndef sortable_lcc_to_short_lcc(lcc: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    As close to the inverse of make_sortable_lcc as possible\n    \"\"\"\n    m = LCC_PARTS_RE.match(lcc)\n    assert m, f'Unable to parse LCC \"{lcc}\"'\n    parts = m.groupdict()\n    parts['letters'] = parts['letters'].strip('-')\n    parts['number'] = parts['number'].strip('0').strip('.')  # Need to do in order!\n    parts['cutter1'] = parts['cutter1'].strip(' ') if parts['cutter1'] else ''\n    parts['rest'] = ' ' + parts['rest'].strip() if parts['rest'] else ''\n\n    return '%(letters)s%(number)s%(cutter1)s%(rest)s' % parts\n\n\ndef clean_raw_lcc(raw_lcc: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Remove noise in lcc before matching to LCC_PARTS_RE\n    \"\"\"\n    lcc = collapse_multiple_space(raw_lcc.replace('\\\\', ' ').strip(' '))\n    if (lcc.startswith('[') and lcc.endswith(']')) or (\n        lcc.startswith('(') and lcc.endswith(')')\n    ):\n        lcc = lcc[1:-1]\n    return lcc\n\n\ndef normalize_lcc_prefix(prefix: str) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"\n    :param str prefix: An LCC prefix\n    :return: Prefix transformed to be a prefix for sortable LCC\n\n    >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123')\n    'A--0123'\n    >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123.')\n    'A--0123'\n    >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123.0')\n    'A--0123.0'\n    >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123.C')\n    'A--0123.00000000.C'\n    >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('A123.C0')\n    'A--0123.00000000.C0'\n    >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('E--')\n    'E--'\n    >>> normalize_lcc_prefix('PN-')\n    'PN-'\n    \"\"\"\n    if re.match(r'^[A-Z]+$', prefix, re.I):\n        return prefix\n    else:\n        lcc_norm = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(prefix.rstrip('.'))\n        if lcc_norm:\n            result = lcc_norm.rstrip('0')\n            if '.' in prefix and prefix.endswith('0'):\n                zeros_to_add = len(prefix) - len(prefix.rstrip('0'))\n                result += '0' * zeros_to_add\n            elif result.endswith('-0000.'):\n                result = result.rstrip('0.')\n            return result.rstrip('.')\n        else:\n            return None\n\n\ndef normalize_lcc_range(start: str, end: str) -> list[str | None]:\n    \"\"\"\n    :param str start: LCC prefix to start range\n    :param str end: LCC prefix to end range\n    :return: range with prefixes being prefixes for sortable LCCs\n    \"\"\"\n    return [\n        lcc if lcc == '*' else short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(lcc) for lcc in (start, end)\n    ]\n\n\ndef choose_sorting_lcc(sortable_lccs: Iterable[str]) -> str:\n    # Choose longest; theoretically most precise?\n    # Note we go to short-form first, so eg 'A123' beats 'A'\n    def short_len(lcc: str) -> int:\n        return len(sortable_lcc_to_short_lcc(lcc))\n\n    return sorted(sortable_lccs, key=short_len, in prefix and prefix.endswith('0'):\n                zeros_to_add = len(prefix) - len(prefix.rstrip('0'))\n                result += '0' * zeros_to_add\n            elif result.endswith('-0000.'):\n \"\"\"\n    :param str start: LCC prefix to start range\n    :param str end: LCC prefix to end range\n    :return: range with prefixes being prefixes for sortable LCCs\n    \"\"\"\n    return [\n        lcc if lcc == '*' else short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(lcc) for lcc in (start, end)\n    ]\n\n\ndef choose_sorting_lcc(sortable_lccs: Iterable[str]) -> str:\n    # Choose longest; theoretically most precise?\n    # Note we go to short-form first, so eg 'A123' beats 'A'\n    def short_len(lcc: str) -> int:\n        return len(sortable_lcc_to_short_lcc(lcc))\n\n    return sorted(sortable_lccs, key=short_len, reverse=True)[0]\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
