Fields that should be rewritten\n    facet_rewrites: dict[tuple[str, str], str]\n\n    def is_search_field(self, field: str):\n        return field in self.all_fields or field in self.field_name_map\n\n    def process_user_sort(self, user_sort: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Convert a user-provided sort to a solr sort\n\n        >>> from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.schemes.works import WorkSearchScheme\n        >>> scheme = WorkSearchScheme()\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('editions')\n        'edition_count desc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('editions, new')\n        'edition_count desc,first_publish_year desc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('random')\n        'random_1 asc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('random_custom_seed')\n        'random_custom_seed asc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('random_custom_seed desc')\n        'random_custom_seed desc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('random_custom_seed asc')\n        'random_custom_seed asc'\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def process_individual_sort(sort: str):\n            if sort.startswith('random_'):\n                # Allow custom randoms; so anything random_* is allowed\n                return sort if ' ' in sort else f'{sort} asc'\n            else:\n                solr_sort = self.sorts[sort]\n                return solr_sort() if callable(solr_sort) else solr_sort\n\n        return ','.join(\n            process_individual_sort(s.strip()) for s in user_sort.split(',')\n        )\n\n    def process_user_query(self, q_param: str) -> str:\n        if q_param == '*:*':\n            # If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\n            luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\n        else:\n            parent.children = new_children\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Not supported for generic class Item\")\n\n\ndef luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\n\n    Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\n    modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\n\n    :param item: Node to traverse\n    :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\n    \"\"\"\n    parents = _parents or []\n    yield item, parents\n    new_parents = [*parents, item]\n    for child in item.children:\n        yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\n\n\ndef escape_unknown_fields(\n    query: str,\n    is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\n    lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar   blah:bar baz:boo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar   blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'TITLE'}.__contains__, lower=False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('hi', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'hi'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('(title:foo) OR (blah:bah)', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    '(title:foo) OR (blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bah)'\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n    # Note we use the string of the tree, because it strips spaces\n    # like: \"title : foo\" -> \"title:foo\"\n    escaped_query = str(tree)\n    offset = 0\n    for sf, _ in fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = re.sub(r'[\\[\\]\\(\\)\\{\\}:\"\\-+?~^/\\\\,]', r'\\\\\\g<0>', escaped)\n    # Remove boolean operators by making them lowercase\n    escaped = re.sub(r'AND|OR|NOT', lambda _1: _1.group(0).lower(), escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n    'title:(foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n    'title:(YES and)'\n    \"\"\"\n    result = ''\n    if escaped:\n        result += f' {op} '.join(\n            f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\n        )\n    if unescaped:\n        if result:\n            result += f' {op} '\n        result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n    return result\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":86,"oldLines":7,"newStart":86,"newLines":7,"lines":["         if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(","             sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name","         ):","-            field = sf.name + r'\\:'","+            field = sf.name + r'\\\\:'","             if hasattr(sf, 'head'):","                 # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;","                 # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing"]},{"oldStart":98,"oldLines":9,"newStart":98,"newLines":9,"lines":["             escaped_query = (","                 escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]","                 + field","-                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 1 + offset :]","+                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 2 + offset :]","             )","-            offset += 1","+            offset += 2","     return escaped_query"," "," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

1 + offset :]\n            )\n            offset += 1","originalFile":"from typing import Callable, Literal, Optional\nfrom luqum.parser import parser\nfrom luqum.tree import Item, SearchField, BaseOperation, Group, Word, Unary\nimport re\n\n\nclass EmptyTreeError(Exception):\n    pass\n\n\ndef luqum_remove_child(child: Item, parents: list[Item]):\n    \"\"\"\n    Removes a child from a luqum parse tree. If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\n            luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\n        else:\n            parent.children = new_children\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Not supported for generic class Item\")\n\n\ndef luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\n\n    Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\n    modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\n\n    :param item: Node to traverse\n    :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\n    \"\"\"\n    parents = _parents or []\n    yield item, parents\n    new_parents = [*parents, item]\n    for child in item.children:\n        yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\n\n\ndef escape_unknown_fields(\n    query: str,\n    is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\n    lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar   blah:bar baz:boo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar   blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'TITLE'}.__contains__, lower=False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('hi', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'hi'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('(title:foo) OR (blah:bah)', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    '(title:foo) OR (blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bah)'\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n    # Note we use the string of the tree, because it strips spaces\n    # like: \"title : foo\" -> \"title:foo\"\n    escaped_query = str(tree)\n    offset = 0\n    for sf, _ in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(\n            sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name\n        ):\n            field = sf.name + r'\\\\:'\n            if hasattr(sf, 'head'):\n                # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;\n                # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing\n                # together\n                field = sf.head + field\n\n            # We will be moving left to right, so we need to adjust the offset\n            # to account for the characters we have already replaced\n            escaped_query = (\n                escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]\n                + field\n                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 2 + offset :]\n            )\n            offset += 2\n    return escaped_query\n\n\ndef fully_escape_query(query: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Try to convert a query to basically a plain lucene string.\n\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = re.sub(r'[\\[\\]\\(\\)\\{\\}:\"\\-+?~^/\\\\,]', r'\\\\\\g<0>', escaped)\n    # Remove boolean operators by making them lowercase\n    escaped = re.sub(r'AND|OR|NOT', lambda _1: _1.group(0).lower(), escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n    'title:(foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n    'title:(YES and)'\n    \"\"\"\n    result = ''\n    if escaped:\n        result += f' {op} '.join(\n            f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\n        )\n    if unescaped:\n        if result:\n            result += f' {op} '\n        result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n    return result\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":86,"oldLines":7,"newStart":86,"newLines":7,"lines":["         if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(","             sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name","         ):","-            field = sf.name + r'\\\\:'","+            field = sf.name + r'\\:'","             if hasattr(sf, 'head'):","                 # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;","                 # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing"]},{"oldStart":98,"oldLines":9,"newStart":98,"newLines":9,"lines":["             escaped_query = (","                 escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]","                 + field","-                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 2 + offset :]","+                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 1 + offset :]","             )","-            offset += 2","+            offset += 1","     return escaped_query"," "," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

2 + offset :]\n            )\n            offset += 2","originalFile":"from typing import Callable, Literal, Optional\nfrom luqum.parser import parser\nfrom luqum.tree import Item, SearchField, BaseOperation, Group, Word, Unary\nimport re\n\n\nclass EmptyTreeError(Exception):\n    pass\n\n\ndef luqum_remove_child(child: Item, parents: list[Item]):\n    \"\"\"\n    Removes a child from a luqum parse tree. If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\n            luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\n        else:\n            parent.children = new_children\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Not supported for generic class Item\")\n\n\ndef luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\n\n    Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\n    modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\n\n    :param item: Node to traverse\n    :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\n    \"\"\"\n    parents = _parents or []\n    yield item, parents\n    new_parents = [*parents, item]\n    for child in item.children:\n        yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\n\n\ndef escape_unknown_fields(\n    query: str,\n    is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\n    lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar   blah:bar baz:boo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar   blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'TITLE'}.__contains__, lower=False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('hi', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'hi'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('(title:foo) OR (blah:bah)', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    '(title:foo) OR (blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bah)'\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n    # Note we use the string of the tree, because it strips spaces\n    # like: \"title : foo\" -> \"title:foo\"\n    escaped_query = str(tree)\n    offset = 0\n    for sf, _ in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(\n            sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name\n        ):\n            field = sf.name + r'\\:'\n            if hasattr(sf, 'head'):\n                # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;\n                # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing\n                # together\n                field = sf.head + field\n\n            # We will be moving left to right, so we need to adjust the offset\n            # to account for the characters we have already replaced\n            escaped_query = (\n                escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]\n                + field\n                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 1 + offset :]\n            )\n            offset += 1\n    return escaped_query\n\n\ndef fully_escape_query(query: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Try to convert a query to basically a plain lucene string.\n\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = re.sub(r'[\\[\\]\\(\\)\\{\\}:\"\\-+?~^/\\\\,]', r'\\\\\\g<0>', escaped)\n    # Remove boolean operators by making them lowercase\n    escaped = re.sub(r'AND|OR|NOT', lambda _1: _1.group(0).lower(), escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n    'title:(foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n    'title:(YES and)'\n    \"\"\"\n    result = ''\n    if escaped:\n        result += f' {op} '.join(\n            f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\n        )\n    if unescaped:\n        if result:\n            result += f' {op} '\n        result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n    return result\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":86,"oldLines":7,"newStart":86,"newLines":7,"lines":["         if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(","             sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name","         ):","-            field = sf.name + r'\\:'","+            field = sf.name + r'\\\\:'","             if hasattr(sf, 'head'):","                 # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;","                 # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing"]},{"oldStart":98,"oldLines":9,"newStart":98,"newLines":9,"lines":["             escaped_query = (","                 escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]","                 + field","-                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 1 + offset :]","+                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 2 + offset :]","             )","-            offset += 1","+            offset += 2","     return escaped_query"," "," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HRbN29qSQHU2YxA3EqXwnc","type":"tool_result","content":"   130→def luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n   131→    \"\"\"\n   132→    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n   133→\n   134→    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n   135→    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n   136→\n   137→    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n   138→    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n   139→\n   140→    Query : title:foo bar\n   141→    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n   142→    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n   143→\n   144→    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n   145→    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n   146→    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n   147→\n   148→    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n   149→    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n   150→\n   151→    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n   152→    \"\"\"\n   153→    tree = parser.parse(query)\n   154→\n   155→    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n   156→        if isinstance(item, Word):\n   157→            return item, None\n   158→        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n   159→            return item.children[0], item\n   160→        else:\n   161→            return None\n   162→\n   163→    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n   164→        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n   165→            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n   166→            # we bundle them together\n   167→            last_sf: SearchField = None\n   168→            to_rem = []\n   169→            for child in node.children:\n   170→                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n   171→                    last_sf = child\n   172→                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n   173→                    word, parent_op = next_word\n   174→                    # Add it over\n   175→                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n   176→                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n   177→                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n   178→                        word.tail = ''\n   179→                    else:\n   180→                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n   181→                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n   182→                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n   183→                        word.tail = ''\n   184→                    if parent_op:\n   185→                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n   186→                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n   187→                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n   188→                        node.op = parent_op.op\n   189→                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n   190→                    to_rem.append(child)\n   191→                else:\n   192→                    last_sf = None\n   193→            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n   194→                # We only have the searchfield left!\n   195→                if parents:\n   196→                    # Move the head to the next element\n   197→                    last_sf.head = node.head\n   198→                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n   199→                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n   200→                        for child in parents[-1].children\n   201→                    )\n   202→                else:\n   203→                    tree = last_sf\n   204→                    break\n   205→            else:\n   206→                node.children = tuple(\n   207→                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n   208→                )\n   209→\n   210→    # Remove spaces before field names\n   211→    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n   212→        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n   213→            node.expr.head = ''\n   214→\n   215→    return tree\n   216→\n   217→\n   218→def query_dict_to_str(\n   219→    escaped: dict | None = None,\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,","numLines":90,"startLine":130,"totalLines":247}}}

If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\n            luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\n        else:\n            parent.children = new_children\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Not supported for generic class Item\")\n\n\ndef luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\n\n    Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\n    modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\n\n    :param item: Node to traverse\n    :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\n    \"\"\"\n    parents = _parents or []\n    yield item, parents\n    new_parents = [*parents, item]\n    for child in item.children:\n        yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\n\n\ndef escape_unknown_fields(\n    query: str,\n    is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\n    lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar   blah:bar baz:boo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar   blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar', fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? Set of queries that define the universe of this scheme\n    universe: list[str]\n    # All actual solr fields that can be in a user query\n    all_fields: set[str]\n    # These fields are fetched for facets and can also be url params\n    facet_fields: set[str]\n    # Mapping of user-only fields to solr fields\n    field_name_map: dict[str, str]\n    # Mapping of user sort to solr sort\n    sorts: dict[str, Union[str, Callable[[], str]]]\n    # Default\n    default_fetched_fields: set[str]\n    # Fields that should be rewritten\n    facet_rewrites: dict[tuple[str, str], str]\n\n    def is_search_field(self, field: str):\n        return field in self.all_fields or field in self.field_name_map\n\n    def process_user_sort(self, user_sort: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Convert a user-provided sort to a solr sort\n\n        >>> from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.schemes.works import WorkSearchScheme\n        >>> scheme = WorkSearchScheme()\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('editions')\n        'edition_count desc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('editions, new')\n        'edition_count desc,first_publish_year desc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('random')\n        'random_1 asc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('random_custom_seed')\n        'random_custom_seed asc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('random_custom_seed desc')\n        'random_custom_seed desc'\n        >>> scheme.process_user_sort('random_custom_seed asc')\n        'random_custom_seed asc'\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def process_individual_sort(sort: str):\n            if sort.startswith('random_'):\n                # Allow custom randoms; so anything random_* is allowed\n                return sort if ' ' in sort else f'{sort} asc'\n            else:\n                solr_sort = self.sorts[sort]\n                return solr_sort() if callable(solr_sort) else solr_sort\n\n        return ','.join(\n            process_individual_sort(s.strip()) for s in user_sort.split(',')\n        )\n\n    def process_user_query(self, q_param: str) -> str:\n        if q_param == '*:*':\n            # This is a special solr syntax; don't process\n            return q_param\n\n        try:\n            q_param = escape_unknown_fields(\n                (\n                    # Solr 4+ has support for regexes (eg `key:/foo.*/`)! But for now,\n                    # let's not expose that and escape all '/'. Otherwise\n                    # `key:/works/OL1W` is interpreted as a regex.\n                    q_param.strip()\n                    .replace('/', '\\\\\\\\/')\n                    # Also escape unexposed lucene features\n                    .replace('?', '\\\\\\\\?')\n                    .replace('~', '\\\\\\\\~')\n                    .replace('-', '\\\\\\\\-')\n                ),\n                self.is_search_field,\n                lower=True,\n            )\n            q_tree = luqum_parser(q_param)\n        except ParseError:\n            # This isn't a syntactically valid lucene query\n            logger.warning(\"Invalid lucene query\", exc_info=True)\n            # Escape everything we can\n            q_tree = luqum_parser(fully_escape_query(q_param))\n\n        q_tree = self.transform_user_query(q_param, q_tree)\n        return str(q_tree)\n\n    def transform_user_query(\n        self,\n        user_query: str,\n        q_tree: luqum.tree.Item,\n    ) -> luqum.tree.Item:\n        return q_tree\n\n    def build_q_from_params(self, params: dict) -> Optional[str]:\n        return None\n\n    def q_to_solr_params(self, q: str, solr_fields: set[str]) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n        return [('q', q)]\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":75,"oldLines":15,"newStart":75,"newLines":17,"lines":["                     # let's not expose that and escape all '/'. This is a special solr syntax; don't process\n            return q_param\n\n        try:\n            q_param = escape_unknown_fields(\n                (\n                    # Solr 4+ has support for regexes (eg `key:/foo.*/`)! But for now,\n                    # let's not expose that and escape all '/'. Otherwise\n                    # `key:/works/OL1W` is interpreted as a regex.\n                    q_param.strip()\n                    .replace('/', '\\\\/')\n                    # Also escape unexposed lucene features\n                    .replace('?', '\\\\?')\n                    .replace('~', '\\\\~')\n                    .replace('-', '\\\\-')\n                ),\n                self.is_search_field,\n                lower=True,\n            )\n            # Double the backslashes to escape them for Solr\n            q_param = q_param.replace('\\\\', '\\\\\\\\')\n            q_tree = luqum_parser(q_param)\n        except ParseError:\n            # This isn't a syntactically valid lucene query\n            logger.warning(\"Invalid lucene query\", exc_info=True)\n            # Escape everything we can\n            q_tree = luqum_parser(fully_escape_query(q_param))\n\n        q_tree = self.transform_user_query(q_param, q_tree)\n        return str(q_tree)\n\n    def transform_user_query(\n        self,\n        user_query: str,\n        q_tree: luqum.tree.Item,\n    ) -> luqum.tree.Item:\n        return q_tree\n\n    def build_q_from_params(self, params: dict) -> Optional[str]:\n        return None\n\n    def q_to_solr_params(self, q: str, solr_fields: set[str]) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n        return [('q', q)]\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":69,"oldLines":23,"newStart":69,"newLines":21,"lines":["             return q_param"," ","         try:","+            # If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\n            luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\n        else:\n            parent.children = new_children\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Not supported for generic class Item\")\n\n\ndef luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\n\n    Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\n    modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\n\n    :param item: Node to traverse\n    :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\n    \"\"\"\n    parents = _parents or []\n    yield item, parents\n    new_parents = [*parents, item]\n    for child in item.children:\n        yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\n\n\ndef escape_unknown_fields(\n    query: str,\n    is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\n    lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar   blah:bar baz:boo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar   blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'TITLE'}.__contains__, lower=False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('hi', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'hi'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('(title:foo) OR (blah:bah)', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    '(title:foo) OR (blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bah)'\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n    # Note we use the string of the tree, because it strips spaces\n    # like: \"title : foo\" -> \"title:foo\"\n    escaped_query = str(tree)\n    offset = 0\n    for sf, _ in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(\n            sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name\n        ):\n            field = sf.name + r'\\:'\n            if hasattr(sf, 'head'):\n                # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;\n                # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing\n                # together\n                field = sf.head + field\n\n            # We will be moving left to right, so we need to adjust the offset\n            # to account for the characters we have already replaced\n            escaped_query = (\n                escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]\n                + field\n                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 1 + offset :]\n            )\n            offset += 1\n    return escaped_query\n\n\ndef fully_escape_query(query: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Try to convert a query to basically a plain lucene string.\n\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n    'title:(foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n    'title:(YES and)'\n    \"\"\"\n    result = ''\n    if escaped:\n        result += f' {op} '.join(\n            f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\n        )\n    if unescaped:\n        if result:\n            result += f' {op} '\n        result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n    return result\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":86,"oldLines":7,"newStart":86,"newLines":7,"lines":["         if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(","             sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name","         ):","-            field = sf.name + r'\\:'","+            field = sf.name + r'\\\\:'","             if hasattr(sf, 'head'):","                 # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;","                 # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\n            luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\n        else:\n            parent.children = new_children\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Not supported for generic class Item\")\n\n\ndef luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\n\n    Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\n    modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\n\n    :param item: Node to traverse\n    :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\n    \"\"\"\n    parents = _parents or []\n    yield item, parents\n    new_parents = [*parents, item]\n    for child in item.children:\n        yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\n\n\ndef escape_unknown_fields(\n    query: str,\n    is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\n    lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar   blah:bar baz:boo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar   blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'title:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo bar baz:boo', {'TITLE'}.__contains__, lower=False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('hi', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    'hi'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('(title:foo) OR (blah:bah)', {'title'}.__contains__)\n    '(title:foo) OR (blah\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:bah)'\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n    # Note we use the string of the tree, because it strips spaces\n    # like: \"title : foo\" -> \"title:foo\"\n    escaped_query = str(tree)\n    offset = 0\n    for sf, _ in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(\n            sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name\n        ):\n            field = sf.name + r'\\\\:'\n            if hasattr(sf, 'head'):\n                # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;\n                # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing\n                # together\n                field = sf.head + field\n\n            # We will be moving left to right, so we need to adjust the offset\n            # to account for the characters we have already replaced\n            escaped_query = (\n                escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]\n                + field\n                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 2 + offset :]\n            )\n            offset += 2\n    return escaped_query\n\n\ndef fully_escape_query(query: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Try to convert a query to basically a plain lucene string.\n\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = re.sub(r'[\\[\\]\\(\\)\\{\\}:\"\\-+?~^/\\\\,]', r'\\\\\\g<0>', escaped)\n    # Remove boolean operators by making them lowercase\n    escaped = re.sub(r'AND|OR|NOT', lambda _1: _1.group(0).lower(), escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            # if any of the children are SearchField followed by one or more words,\n            # we bundle them together\n            last_sf: SearchField = None\n            to_rem = []\n            for child in node.children:\n                if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):\n                    last_sf = child\n                elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):\n                    word, parent_op = next_word\n                    # Add it over\n                    if not isinstance(last_sf.expr, Group):\n                        last_sf.expr = Group(type(node)(last_sf.expr, word))\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    else:\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children[-1].tail = last_sf.expr.tail\n                        last_sf.expr.expr.children += (word,)\n                        last_sf.expr.tail = word.tail\n                        word.tail = ''\n                    if parent_op:\n                        # A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n    'title:(foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n    'title:(YES and)'\n    \"\"\"\n    result = ''\n    if escaped:\n        result += f' {op} '.join(\n            f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\n        )\n    if unescaped:\n        if result:\n            result += f' {op} '\n        result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n    return result\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":86,"oldLines":7,"newStart":86,"newLines":7,"lines":["         if isinstance(sf, SearchField) and not is_valid_field(","             sf.name.lower() if lower else sf.name","         ):","-            field = sf.name + r'\\\\:'","+            field = sf.name + r'\\:'","             if hasattr(sf, 'head'):","                 # head and tail are used for whitespace between fields;","                 # copy it along to the write space to avoid things smashing"]},{"oldStart":98,"oldLines":9,"newStart":98,"newLines":9,"lines":["             escaped_query = (","                 escaped_query[: sf.pos + offset]","                 + field","-                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 2 + offset :]","+                + escaped_query[sf.pos + len(field) - 1 + offset :]","             )","-            offset += 2","+            offset += 1","     return escaped_query"," "," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

If the tree\n    ends up being empty, errors.\n\n    :param child: Node to remove\n    :param parents: Path of parent nodes leading from the root of the tree\n    \"\"\"\n    parent = parents[-1] if parents else None\n    if parent is None:\n        # We cannot remove the element if it is the root of the tree\n        raise EmptyTreeError()\n    elif isinstance(parent, (BaseOperation, Group, Unary)):\n        new_children = tuple(c for c in parent.children if c != child)\n        if not new_children:\n            # If we have deleted all the children, we need to delete the parent\n            # as well. And potentially recurse up the tree.\n            luqum_remove_child(parent, parents[:-1])\n        else:\n            parent.children = new_children\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Not supported for generic class Item\")\n\n\ndef luqum_traverse(item: Item, _parents: list[Item] | None = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Traverses every node in the parse tree in depth-first order.\n\n    Does not make any guarantees about what will happen if you\n    modify the tree while traversing it 😅 But we do it anyways.\n\n    :param item: Node to traverse\n    :param _parents: Internal parameter for tracking parents\n    \"\"\"\n    parents = _parents or []\n    yield item, parents\n    new_parents = [*parents, item]\n    for child in item.children:\n        yield from luqum_traverse(child, new_parents)\n\n\ndef escape_unknown_fields(\n    query: str,\n    is_valid_field: Callable[[str], bool],\n    lower=True,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Escapes the colon of any search field that is not deemed valid by the\n    predicate function `is_valid_field`.\n\n    :param query: Query to escape\n    :param is_valid_field: Predicate function that determines if a field is valid\n    :param lower: If true, the field will be lowercased before being checked\n\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo', lambda field: False)\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> escape_unknown_fields('title:foo fully_escape_query('title:foo')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo bar')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo bar'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('title:foo (bar baz:boo)')\n    'title\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(bar baz\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:boo\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\)'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('x:[A TO Z}')\n    'x\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\:\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\[A TO Z\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}'\n    >>> fully_escape_query('foo AND bar')\n    'foo and bar'\n    \"\"\"\n    escaped = query\n    # Escape special characters\n    escaped = escaped)\n    return escaped\n\n\ndef luqum_parser(query: str) -> Item:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses a lucene-like query, with the special binding rules of Open Library.\n\n    In our queries, unlike native solr/lucene, field names are greedy, and\n    affect the rest of the query until another field is hit.\n\n    Here are some examples. The first query is the native solr/lucene\n    parsing. The second is the parsing we want.\n\n    Query : title:foo bar\n    Lucene: (title:foo) bar\n    OL    : (title:foo bar)\n\n    Query : title:foo OR bar AND author:blah\n    Lucene: (title:foo) OR (bar) AND (author:blah)\n    OL    : (title:foo OR bar) AND (author:blah)\n\n    This requires an annoying amount of manipulation of the default\n    Luqum parser, unfortunately.\n\n    Also, OL queries allow spaces after fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    tree = parser.parse(query)\n\n    def find_next_word(item: Item) -> Optional[tuple[Word, Optional[BaseOperation]]]:\n        if isinstance(item, Word):\n            return item, None\n        elif isinstance(item, BaseOperation) and isinstance(item.children[0], Word):\n            return item.children[0], item\n        else:\n            return None\n\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, BaseOperation):\n            #
