A query like: 'title:foo blah OR author:bar\n                        # Lucene parses as: (title:foo) ? (blah OR author:bar)\n                        # We want         : (title:foo ? blah) OR (author:bar)\n                        node.op = parent_op.op\n                        node.children += (*parent_op.children[1:],)\n                    to_rem.append(child)\n                else:\n                    last_sf = None\n            if len(to_rem) == len(node.children) - 1:\n                # We only have the searchfield left!\n                if parents:\n                    # Move the head to the next element\n                    last_sf.head = node.head\n                    parents[-1].children = tuple(\n                        child if child is not node else last_sf\n                        for child in parents[-1].children\n                    )\n                else:\n                    tree = last_sf\n                    break\n            else:\n                node.children = tuple(\n                    child for child in node.children if child not in to_rem\n                )\n\n    # Remove spaces before field names\n    for node, parents in luqum_traverse(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, SearchField):\n            node.expr.head = ''\n\n    return tree\n\n\ndef query_dict_to_str(\n    escaped: dict | None = None,\n    unescaped: dict | None = None,\n    op: Literal['AND', 'OR', ''] = '',\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Converts a query dict to a search query.\n\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo'})\n    'title:(foo)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'})\n    'title:(foo bar)  author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo bar', 'author': 'bar'}, op='OR')\n    'title:(foo bar) OR author:(bar)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'foo ? to escape'})\n    'title:(foo \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\? to escape)'\n    >>> query_dict_to_str({'title': 'YES AND'})\n    'title:(YES and)'\n    \"\"\"\n    result = ''\n    if escaped:\n        result += f' {op} '.join(\n            f'{k}:({fully_escape_query(v)})' for k, v in escaped.items()\n        )\n    if unescaped:\n        if result:\n            result += f' {op} '\n        result += f' {op} '.join(f'{k}:{v}' for k, v in unescaped.items())\n    return result\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":168,"oldLines":7,"newStart":168,"newLines":12,"lines":["             to_rem = []","             for child in node.children:","                 if isinstance(child, SearchField) and isinstance(child.expr, Word):","-                    last_sf = child","+                    # Skip bundling for escaped field names (those containing backslashes)","+                    # as they indicate invalid/unknown fields that shouldn't have greedy binding","+                    if '\\\\' not in child.name:","+                        last_sf = child","+                    else:","+                        last_sf = None","                 elif last_sf and (next_word := find_next_word(child)):","                     word, parent_op = next_word","                     # Add it over"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01JiASF2gfJKi9vA9a6Cr5LK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Great progress! We've gone from 3 failing tests to 2. The \"Colons in query\" test is now passing! \n\nBut we still have issues with:1. \"Spaced colons in query\": The space before the escaped colon is being removed. We're getting `flatland\\\\:a` instead of `flatland\\\\: a`2. \"Colons in field\": The field value is not being wrapped in parentheses. We're getting `alternative_title:flatland\\\\:a...` instead of `alternative_title:(flatland\\\\:a...)`\n\nFor issue 2, the problem is that our check for escaped field names (`'\\\\' not in child.name`) is preventing bundling for the valid field `title`. But we need to bundle the words after a valid field!The issue is that when we have `'title:flatland\\\\:a...'`, the parser creates:- UnknownOperation with children:  - SearchField(title) with expr=SearchField(flatland\\\\) with expr=Word(a)  - Word(romance), Word(of), Word(many), Word(dimensions)And our bundling logic only works on SearchFields with Word expr, not nested SearchFields.\n\nSo the real issue is that the nested SearchFields are preventing the bundling logic from working. The solution might be to unwrap the nested SearchFields before bundling.\n\nActually, looking more closely at the tree structure, the issue is that the double-escaped colon is causing a problem. After transformation, all backslashes are doubled for Solr: `foo foo bar \\\\-` and `flatland\\\\:a...`\n\n\n6.
